title: epipactis palustris
intro: Epipactis palustris, also known as marsh helleborine, is a species of orchid that grows in wetland areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Epipactis palustris are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of Epipactis palustris can be made by infusing the roots and leaves in water or alcohol, although it is essential to consult with a medical professional before using the plant for medicinal purposes.
